batches.notes: Sequential rows 2-21 (2-B Energy through AESC Spain). Sources: BusinessWire, GlobeNewsWire, Windpower Monthly, Automation.com, CompositesWorld, ACCIONA pressroom, RenewablesNow, PV-Magazine, Japan Times, La Moncloa. Flags: Aeolon row 17 cites Nador not Tanger; AESC Douai row 18 investment discrepancy ($920M vs ~€2B); AESC Sunderland row 20 investment discrepancy ($622M vs £1B). AAER row 5 Bordeaux/Valorem not independently confirmed — fallback fDi Markets (FT).

Sequential rows 102-121 (VW Hefei R&D through Toshiba Houston). Sources: volkswagen-group.com, govt.chinadaily.com.cn, pv-magazine.com, ess-news.com, gotion.com.cn, koreaherald.com, kedglobal.com, news.metal.com (Sinoma), prnewswire.com (Boston-Power), northvolt.com/scania, cbc.ca (Bolloré), farasis-energy.com, hipa.hu (KunlunChem), yicaiglobal.com (Pret), nec.com, valmet-automotive.com, ledinside.com, argusmedia.com, umicore.com, umicore.kr. Fallback fDi Markets (FT) for R120 (JinYang Malaysia — no Tier A/B source found after company IR + IPA + local-language passes) and R121 (Toshiba Houston — no public announcement found for $85M 2025 plant). A-O reconciliations: R105 G Hanoi→Ha Tinh (Gotion press release confirms Ha Tinh/Vung Ang; S flagged [UNVERIFIED] — $140M is approx Gotion 51% share of $275M total); R112 G Debrecen→Szolnok (HIPA confirms KunlunChem facility is in Szolnok, not Debrecen); R113 G Kuala Lumpur→Johor (batteriesnews confirms Johor state for Pret/Highstar factory); R115 I None→2023 (Valmet Automotive Kirchardt series production started Feb 2023); R117 G Jakarta→Kendal (Kendal SEZ Central Java, confirmed by Kendal Industrial Park and Argus Media); R118 M 0→1 / N None→1 / O None→Jiangmen Changxin Technology (Umicore groundbreaking press release confirms JUC is a JV with Jiangmen Changxin Technology). S column [UNVERIFIED] applied to R102, R104 (Batam city not confirmed by sources — Weda Bay suggested), R108, R109, R111, R114, R115, R116, R117, R118, R119 where amount was not unambiguously confirmed.
